title: ilex subodorata
intro: Ilex subodorata is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae, commonly known as the bitter yew. It is native to tropical Africa, and its leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves of Ilex subodorata have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Ilex subodorata include tea and infusions made from its leaves, which are used to treat a range of ailments, and it is also used in some traditional remedies to treat infections and skin conditions.
